methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $350M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: Collaborative Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: GrayMatter Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

data.narrative:
Fairmat, a Series B robotics and manufacturing company founded by Benjamin Saada, is valued at $350M post-money following ~$103M in total capital raised. The valuation assessment draws from a peer universe of five private robotics companies at Series B stage (1X Technologies, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Collaborative Robotics, and GrayMatter Robotics), supplemented by one public comparable.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using a typical Series B to next-stage step-up multiplier of 1.9x applied to the $350M base, implies a valuation of $665M. Peer-median analysis across the five same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $1.6B, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B. The wide peer range reflects significant dispersion in the Series B robotics cohort, notably driven by 1X Technologies at $10.0B.

The two-method triangulation yields a valuation range of $565M to $1.9B, with a median cross-section of $1.2B. Fairmat's current $350M valuation sits at the lower bound of the peer range and well below both method-derived estimates. Key uncertainties include the justification for 1X Technologies' $10.0B outlier valuation and whether Fairmat's specific technology, market position, or capital efficiency support positioning toward the median peer or toward the stage-progression benchmark.
